mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing activity and rising capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) following months with above-median INDPRO growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index inflation accelerating above trend forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising the equity discount rate and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when the CPIAUCSL trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment and increases in payroll employment signal robust labor market momentum that boosts aggregate consumption and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal combining UNRATE and PAYEMS does not outperform the stronger univariate signal by at least 2 percentage points in directional accuracy over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns only when the federal funds rate is below one percent, because low financing costs amplify the pass-through from consumer spending to corporate profits over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS is below 1 percent, RSAFS growth fails to generate positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP surprises reveal aggregate demand strength that markets reprice rapidly, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors update earnings expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following above-median GDPC1 releases are not directionally positive in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts accelerating more than five percent over three months signals an early-cycle demand recovery that lifts construction, materials, and household durable sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or statistically insignificant (t-stat < 1.0) during periods when the HOUST trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate greater than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor income and collapsing consumption demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45 percent of triggered instances during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with an elevated federal funds rate creates a stagflationary profit squeeze where falling output and high borrowing costs jointly depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO change and FEDFUNDS level exhibits a rank correlation with 20-day returns below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
